notes.text: Varrock was originally founded around year 700 of the Fourth Age as "Avarrocka", when a nomadic tribe settled near an exceptionally strong young boy they had discovered.

A decade after the settlement formed, the unnamed boy was sent on a quest to earn his name. He encountered goblins attacking the settlement, and when they screamed "arrav" he defeated them and became known as Arrav.

The Zamorakian necromancer Zemouregal targeted the growing city of Avarrocka. Arrav left to seek a mythical shield for protection but returned to find the city burned. It was rebuilt, but about a century into Avarrocka's existence Zemouregal invaded with zombie forces, killing Arrav and retreating with his undead body.
The settlement expanded into the kingdom of Misthalin and eventually became a monarchy.
Creatures from Morytania attacked Varrock until seven priestly warriors defeated them and blessed the River Salve, making it impassable to Zamorakian creatures.

notes.topic: Shield of Arrav and the gangs
notes.text: The Shield of Arrav was stolen by the Phoenix Gang, which later split into the Phoenix Gang and the Black Arm Gang, occupying different districts of the city. An adventurer eventually retrieved the shield and later helped Dimintheis Fitzharmon use its powers against Zemouregal's second invasion.
The original name "Avarrocka" persists among some groups such as barbarians, while other races have their own names for the city.
The Varrock wiki Transportation section does not list specific prices for any teleport spell, jewellery teleport, or transport system.
Aubury, in addition to running the rune shop, teleports players to the Rune Essence mine.
The Estate Agent in Varrock provides player-owned house services such as relocation and redecoration. Modeled as an NPC/activity rather than a buy/sell shop.
The Hairdresser's is run by Emma and Suzie and offers a free makeover service (not a buy/sell shop).
Thessalia's Fine Clothes also offers free appearance changes and event item reclamation in addition to selling robes, capes, gloves, and boots.

Verbatim: "Players who have completed tiers of the Varrock Diary may buy noted battlestaves at a discounted price of 7,000 coins each once per day from the barrel on the ground floor." Daily stock scales by tier: Easy 15, Medium 30, Hard 60, Elite 120 (each at 7,000 coins). Profitable because the Grand Exchange price typically exceeds the 7,000 shop price.
Most Varrockian pubs sell only beer. No prices are specified on the Varrock page for any pub items.
Ratpit Bar is a very little known, very small pub located down a manhole in south-east Varrock within a cordoned-off area, occupied mostly by gamblers taking breaks from the Rat Pits minigame.
The Jolly Boar Inn is a large inn located outside the city walls (north-east of Varrock).
The Blue Moon Inn is described as the most popular pub in Varrock; NPCs present include Dr Harlow (former Slayer master) and Johnny the Beard.
Large facility south of the Grand Exchange, popular for Smithing training due to its proximity to an anvil.
Small two-story bank south of the Varrock Museum.
Prestigious guild offering tough battles, rune weapons and green dragonhide armour.
Includes a pie shop and a wheat grinder; requires 32 Cooking to enter.
Features a natural history quiz, fossil displays, and a kudos system.
Located on the 1st floor toward the rear of the palace.
In the chapel at the north-east corner of Varrock.
Prayer altar in the Zamorakian chapel south of Aubury's Rune Shop.
Three anvils: south of the west bank, north-west of the east bank inside Horvik's Armour Shop, and south of the east bank.
Contains tin, copper, and iron rocks.
Near the Champions' Guild; contains clay, tin, silver, and iron rocks.
Large sewer system that hosts monsters and bosses such as Scurrius and Bryophyta.
Large mansion where Juliet Leptoc resides.
Building north of the east bank with attackable dummies for Attack training to level 8.
Palace with a queen's garden and library; the palace altar is on the 1st floor toward the rear.
No furnace, spinning wheel, loom, or pottery wheel was explicitly listed for Varrock in the fetched Locations section.
Lucien (level 14) is only fought during Temple of Ikov.
Giant rat appears at level 3 or 6.
The Varrock Herald costs 50 coins.
Aubury teleports players to the Rune Essence mine.
A prestigious guild offering tough battles, rune weapons and green dragonhide armour; no resident NPC details given in the Inhabitants section.
